text: Sex differences exist in predictors of ultramarathon performance, physiological responses to training/racing/recovery, chronic and acute health issues, and pacing strategies.
Most ultramarathon training, racing, nutrition, and recovery guidelines are based on research that largely excluded female athletes.
Sex-specific guidelines for ultramarathon coaching could significantly improve performance and health outcomes for female runners.
Current evidence on sex differences in ultramarathon running is predominantly observational and of low overall quality, with only 3 RCTs identified across 45 studies.
Male and female ultramarathon runners exhibit different pacing strategies during races.
